raw_consumer_statement:
Complaint Against : American Express ( Amex ) Product/Service : Credit Card Travel/Baggage Insurance Description of Complaint : I am filing a complaint regarding unfair charges placed on my American Express account related to baggage and flight insurance. 

Over 23 years ago, I was initially enrolled in an Amex insurance program for travel/baggage coverage. Since that time, I never used the coverage, and for decades no charges were applied to my account. Recently, on a new flight purchase, Amex automatically charged me for this insurance without ever notifying me in advance, giving me updated disclosures, or providing me the opportunity to continue or deny the coverage. 

I spoke with XXXX, a supervisor with Amex Insurance on XX/XX/year>, who was reluctant to credit my account for multiple charges, despite my clear objection and request. I was never informed that this insurance would automatically remain in effect indefinitely, nor was I given any annual reminders, notices, or opt-out options. 

This practice appears to be in violation of consumer protection laws. Under the federal Truth in Lending Act ( Regulation Z ), consumers are supposed to be protected from unfair or unauthorized charges on their accounts. Automatically charging for insurance coverage after more than two decades without renewed consent or disclosure is both unfair and deceptive.

I request the CFPB investigate this matter and ensure that : These charges are fully reversed on my account. 

Amex is required to provide proper disclosures and obtain clear consent from customers before enrolling or reactivating charges for insurance products. 

Consumers are protected from being automatically billed for stale enrollments from decades earlier.
